evals.prompt: I want to build a Python CLI that converts times between timezones. It will be 100% TDD. The audience is developers who need reliable timezone conversion in scripts.
evals.expected_output: Three files written with status approved: prd.md (business document), spec.md (technical execution plan with TDD strategy), and gherkin-spec.md (BDD scenarios). All gates approved before advancing.
assertions: prd.md exists at .orchestra/work/{id}/prd.md
prd.md frontmatter has status: approved
prd.md contains a ## Problem section
prd.md contains a ## Objective section
prd.md contains a ## Success Criteria section with at least 2 checkboxes
prd.md contains no framework or library names (pytest, click, argparse, zoneinfo, pytz, etc.)
prd.md contains no file paths in the narrative body
prd.md contains no technical implementation language
spec.md exists at .orchestra/work/{id}/spec.md
spec.md frontmatter has status: approved
spec.md contains a ## Testing Strategy section
spec.md contains a ### Unit Tests subsection
spec.md contains a ### Integration Tests subsection
spec.md contains a ### E2E Tests subsection
spec.md contains an ## Acceptance Criteria section
spec.md acceptance criteria are split into Functional, Unit, Integration, and E2E subsections
gherkin-spec.md exists at .orchestra/work/{id}/gherkin-spec.md
gherkin-spec.md contains at least one Scenario with Given/When/Then
gherkin-spec.md contains at least one error or negative scenario
gherkin-spec.md Feature name matches spec title

evals.prompt: I need to build a REST API for managing a reading list. Users can add books, mark them read, and get recommendations. Python, TDD.
evals.expected_output: PRD focused on user value (not API design), spec with TDD strategy across all three tiers, Gherkin scenarios covering happy path and error cases.
assertions: prd.md ## Problem section describes a user problem, not a system problem
prd.md ## Objective is written from user perspective, not implementation perspective
prd.md ## Success Criteria are observable by a non-technical stakeholder
prd.md contains no HTTP method names (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
prd.md contains no endpoint paths (/api/, /books/, etc.)
prd.md contains no database or ORM references
spec.md ## Testing Strategy integration section states tests hit real HTTP endpoints, not mocks
spec.md ## Risks section has at least one risk with a mitigation
gherkin-spec.md has 3 or more scenarios
gherkin-spec.md has at least one error scenario (invalid input, not found, etc.)

evals.expected_output: Skill reads existing work item state, determines which stage is complete, and continues from the next unapproved stage without regenerating already-approved artifacts.
assertions: Skill reads .orchestra/work/core-logic/ before generating anything
Skill does not overwrite an artifact with status: approved
Skill continues from the first incomplete stage
If all three artifacts exist with status approved, skill reports planning complete and stops
